stimulus:
The following text is adapted from a 2021 short story by Renata Cruz. The narrator has just stepped onstage for her first school recital.

The stage lights were brighter than anything I had ever stood under before. My fingers tingled on the keys, and even my breath felt too loud. I was nervous, but I was also proud.
stem: According to the text, how does the narrator feel about being on the recital stage?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer because the narrator directly says she was "nervous" but also "proud," capturing both feelings.

Choice B is incorrect; "nervous" is not the same as overjoyed and the text balances pride with nervousness. Choice C is incorrect; the text says nothing about bitterness or embarrassment. Choice D is incorrect; the description of tingling fingers and loud breathing is the opposite of calm.
